sections.heading: Surgery
sections.content: Surgical resection is the primary treatment for localized (Stage I-III) SBA and may be considered for select patients with metastatic disease (metastasectomy). The goal is complete (R0) resection with adequate lymph node dissection.

cards.name: Pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple)
cards.detail: Approach: Open or minimally invasive (by experienced surgeons). Indication: Should be considered for all duodenal malignancies, particularly if arising in the second portion of the duodenum or invading any portion of the ampulla or pancreas. Pylorus preservation is acceptable in the absence of a hereditary condition. Key complications: Pancreatic fistula, delayed gastric emptying, bleeding, infection. Operative mortality: Not specified in source text.

cards.name: Limited Segmentectomy (Duodenum)
cards.detail: Approach: Segmental resection. Indication: Controversial. May be considered for select cases in the absence of a hereditary condition, particularly in lesions on the anti-mesenteric side of the intestine that involve the third and fourth segments of the duodenum. Case reports suggest it may be considered for lesions of the first portion, particularly those <2 cm in size and located on the mesenteric side. Key complications: May have lower lymph node yield; potential for positive margins if not carefully performed. Operative mortality: Not specified in source text.

cards.name: Segmentectomy (Jejunum/Ileum)
cards.detail: Approach: Segmental resection. Indication: Standard for jejunal and ileal tumors. Key complications: Standard surgical risks (bleeding, infection, anastomotic leak). Operative mortality: Not specified in source text.

cards.name: Terminal Ileal Resection with Right Hemicolectomy
cards.detail: Approach: Open or minimally invasive. Indication: Should be used for distal ileal tumors. Key complications: Standard right colon resection risks. Operative mortality: Not specified in source text.
